Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class OptionOrderCreate

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.OptionOrderCreate
public class OptionOrderCreate extends Object
OptionOrderCreate

The message describing an option order

    The execution type of this option order.

    The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

  • Constructor Details

    • OptionOrderCreate

      public OptionOrderCreate(OptionOrderCreateBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity, String clientOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime, String currencyCode, Optional<? extends List<FeeCreate>> fees, List<OptionOrderLegCreate> legs, DecimalCreate limitPrice, DateCreate orderDate, OptionOrderCreateOrderType orderType, PriceDirection priceDirection, DecimalCreate quantity, Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderCreateSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions, OptionOrderCreateTimeInForce timeInForce)

    • OptionOrderCreate

      public OptionOrderCreate(OptionOrderCreateBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity, String clientOrderId, String currencyCode, List<OptionOrderLegCreate> legs, DecimalCreate limitPrice, DateCreate orderDate, OptionOrderCreateOrderType orderType, PriceDirection priceDirection, DecimalCreate quantity, OptionOrderCreateTimeInForce timeInForce)

  • Method Details

    • brokerCapacity

      public OptionOrderCreateBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity()
      The capacity in which the broker is acting for this option order.

    • clientOrderId

      public String clientOrderId()
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime()
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • currencyCode

      public String currencyCode()
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • fees

      public Optional<List<FeeCreate>> fees()
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • legs

      public List<OptionOrderLegCreate> legs()
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • limitPrice

      public DecimalCreate limitPrice()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • orderDate

      public DateCreate orderDate()
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • orderType

      public OptionOrderCreateOrderType orderType()
      The execution type of this option order.

    • priceDirection

      public PriceDirection priceDirection()
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • quantity

      public DecimalCreate quantity()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Optional<List<OptionOrderCreateSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions()
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • timeInForce

      public OptionOrderCreateTimeInForce timeInForce()
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • builder

      public static OptionOrderCreate.Builder builder()

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public OptionOrderCreate withBrokerCapacity(OptionOrderCreateBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity)
      The capacity in which the broker is acting for this option order.

    • withClientOrderId

      public OptionOrderCreate withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OptionOrderCreate withClientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OptionOrderCreate withClientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public OptionOrderCreate withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withFees

      public OptionOrderCreate withFees(List<FeeCreate> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • withFees

      public OptionOrderCreate withFees(Optional<? extends List<FeeCreate>> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • withLegs

      public OptionOrderCreate withLegs(List<OptionOrderLegCreate> legs)
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • withLimitPrice

      public OptionOrderCreate withLimitPrice(DecimalCreate limitPrice)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • withOrderDate

      public OptionOrderCreate withOrderDate(DateCreate orderDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • withOrderType

      public OptionOrderCreate withOrderType(OptionOrderCreateOrderType orderType)
      The execution type of this option order.

    • withPriceDirection

      public OptionOrderCreate withPriceDirection(PriceDirection priceDirection)
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • withQuantity

      public OptionOrderCreate withQuantity(DecimalCreate quantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrderCreate withSpecialReportingInstructions(List<OptionOrderCreateSpecialReportingInstructions> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrderCreate withSpecialReportingInstructions(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderCreateSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withTimeInForce

      public OptionOrderCreate withTimeInForce(OptionOrderCreateTimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.
